questions.question: What is the concentration of a solution containing 2g of NaOH in 100cm³ of solution? [Na = 23, O = 16, H = 1]
questions.explanation: Molar mass of NaOH = 23 + 16 + 1 = 40. Moles = 2/40 = 0.05. Volume = 100 cm³ = 0.1 dm³. Concentration = 0.05/0.1 = 0.50 mol dm⁻³

questions.question: Which of the following properties is NOT peculiar to matter?
options: kinetic energy of particles increases from solid to gas
Random motion of particles increases from liquid to gas
Orderliness of particles increases from gas to liquid
Random motion of particles increases from gas to solid
questions.explanation: Random motion decreases from gas to solid. Gas has highest random motion, then liquid, then solid with least random motion.

questions.question: The principle of column chromatography is based on the ability of the constituents to
options: move at different speeds in the column
dissolve in each other in the column
react with the solvent in the column
react with each other in the column
questions.explanation: Column chromatography separates components based on their different rates of movement through the stationary phase.

questions.question: From the diagram above, an ideal gas can be represented by
Which of the following questions is correct about the periodic table?
options: The non-metallic properties of the elements tend to decrease across each period
The valence electrons of the elements increase progressively across the period
Elements in the same group have the same number of electron shells
Elements in the same period have the same number of valence electrons
questions.explanation: Valence electrons increase from left to right across a period (e.g., Na has 1, Cl has 7).

questions.question: The relative atomic mass of a naturally occurring lithium consisting of 90% ⁷Li and 10% ⁶Li is
An isotope has an atomic number of 15 and a mass number of 31. The number of protons it contains is
questions.explanation: Atomic number equals the number of protons. Therefore, number of protons = 15

questions.question: The molecular lattice of iodine is held together by
questions.explanation: Iodine (I₂) is a molecular substance held together by weak van der Waal's forces between molecules.

questions.question: The arrangement of particles in crystal lattices can be studied using
questions.explanation: X-rays have wavelengths comparable to atomic spacing in crystals, making them ideal for X-ray diffraction studies.

questions.question: From the diagram above, find the amount of solute deposited when 200 cm³ of the solution is cooled from 55°C to 40°C
The importance of sodium aluminate (III) in the treatment of water is to
questions.explanation: Sodium aluminate acts as a coagulant to aggregate colloidal particles, aiding their removal from water.

questions.question: What type of bond exists between an element X with atomic number 12 and Y with atomic number 17?
questions.explanation: X (Mg, 2,8,2) and Y (Cl, 2,8,7) form an ionic/electrovalent bond. Mg donates electrons to Cl.

questions.question: Hardness of water is mainly due to the presence of
options: calcium chloride or sodium chloride salts
questions.explanation: Water hardness is caused by dissolved calcium and magnesium salts, particularly carbonates and sulphates.

questions.question: A suitable solvent for iodine and naphthalene is
questions.explanation: Ethanol dissolves both iodine and naphthalene well, making it a suitable solvent for both non-polar organic compounds.

questions.question: Which of the following noble gases is commonly found in the atmosphere?
questions.explanation: Argon makes up about 0.93% of the atmosphere, much more abundant than other noble gases.

questions.question: N₂O₄(g) ⇋ 2NO₂(g) ΔH = +ve. In the reaction above, an increase in temperature will
options: increase the value of the equilibrium constant
decrease the value of the equilibrium constant
shift the equilibrium to the left
questions.explanation: The reaction is endothermic (+ve ΔH). Increasing temperature favors the forward reaction, increasing Kc.

questions.question: CH₃COOH(aq) + OH⁻(aq) ⇋ CH₃COO⁻(aq) + H₂O(l). In the reaction above, CH₃COO⁻(aq) is
questions.explanation: CH₃COO⁻ is the conjugate base of the weak acid CH₃COOH (it differs by one H⁺).

questions.question: How many cations will be produced from a solution of potassium aluminium tetraoxosulphate (VI)?
questions.explanation: KAl(SO₄)₂ dissociates into K⁺ and Al³⁺, producing 2 types of cations.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT an alkali?
questions.explanation: NH₃ is a weak base but not a true alkali. Alkalies are metal hydroxides; NH₃ is not a hydroxide.

questions.question: An effect of thermal pollution on water bodies is that the
options: level of oxides of nitrogen increases
questions.explanation: Higher temperature reduces oxygen solubility in water, causing oxygen depletion in aquatic ecosystems.

questions.question: Which of the following is a deliquescent compound?
questions.explanation: CaCl₂ is deliquescent—it absorbs moisture from air and dissolves in it.

questions.question: A chemical reaction in which the hydration energy is greater than the lattice energy is referred to as
questions.explanation: When hydration energy > lattice energy, energy is released, making it exothermic.

questions.question: The function of zinc electrode in a galvanic cell is that it
questions.explanation: Zinc is more reactive and acts as the anode, undergoing oxidation to produce electrons.

questions.question: CH₄(g) + Cl₂(g) → CH₃Cl(s) + HCl(g). The major factor that influences the rate of the reaction above is
questions.explanation: This is a free radical substitution reaction initiated by light (photochemical reaction).

questions.question: The condition required for corrosion to take place is the presence of
questions.explanation: Iron corrosion requires both water (electrolyte) and oxygen. CO₂ is not essential.

questions.question: In the diagram above, X is the
The diagram best illustrates the effect of decrease in
MnO₄⁻(aq) + Y + 5Fe²⁺(aq) → Mn²⁺(aq) + 5Fe³⁺(aq) + 4H₂O(l). In the equation above, Y is
questions.explanation: Balancing the redox equation: Mn(+7→+2) gains 5e⁻; 5Fe²⁺ loses 5e⁻. Need 5H⁺ to balance in acidic medium.

questions.question: Given that M is the mass of a substance deposited during electrolysis and Q is the quantity of electricity consumed, then Faraday's first law can be written as
questions.explanation: Faraday's first law: M = EQ, where E is the electrochemical equivalent.

questions.question: The impurities formed during the laboratory preparation of chlorine gas are removed by
questions.explanation: Water removes HCl and other soluble impurities from chlorine gas.

questions.question: The effect of the presence of impurities such as carbon and sulphur on iron is that they
questions.explanation: Carbon and sulphur impurities lower iron's melting point and make it brittle.

questions.question: A few drops of concentrated HNO₃ is added to an unknown solution and boiled for a while. If this produces a brown solution, the cations present are likely to be
questions.explanation: Fe²⁺ is oxidized to Fe³⁺ by HNO₃, producing a brown/yellow Fe³⁺ solution.

questions.question: The bleaching action of chlorine gas is effective due to the presence of
questions.explanation: Chlorine forms HClO in water, which is the actual bleaching agent.

questions.question: In the laboratory preparation of oxygen, dried oxygen is usually collected over
questions.explanation: Concentrated H₂SO₄ is used both as a drying agent and collection medium for oxygen.

questions.question: The property of concentrated H₂SO₄ that makes it suitable for preparing HNO₃ is its
questions.explanation: H₂SO₄'s strong oxidizing properties allow it to oxidize nitrates to produce HNO₃.

questions.question: Bronze is preferred to copper in the making of medals because it
questions.explanation: Bronze (Cu-Sn alloy) is lighter than pure copper, making it more suitable for medals.

questions.question: The constituent of baking powder that makes the dough to rise is
questions.explanation: NaHCO₃ (sodium bicarbonate) decomposes when heated to produce CO₂ gas, causing dough to rise.

questions.question: Which of the following compounds is used as a gaseous fuel?
questions.explanation: Butane (CH₃-CH₂-CH₂-CH₃) is a gaseous fuel commonly used in lighters and stoves.

questions.question: The ability of carbon to form long chains is referred to as
questions.explanation: Catenation is the ability of carbon to bond with itself, forming long chains.

questions.question: Which of the following compounds will undergo polymerization reaction?
questions.explanation: C₂H₄ (ethene) has a C=C double bond and undergoes addition polymerization.

questions.question: An organic compound has an empirical formula CH₂O and vapour density of 45. What is the molecular formula? [C = 12, H = 1, O = 16]
questions.explanation: Molar mass = 2 × 45 = 90. Empirical formula mass = 30. n = 90/30 = 3. Molecular formula = (CH₂O)₃ = C₃H₆O₃

questions.question: C₆H₁₂O₆ → 2C₂H₅OH + 2CO₂ + energy at 25°C. The reaction represented by the equation above is useful in the production of
questions.explanation: This is fermentation of glucose, producing ethanol and CO₂.

questions.question: The number of isomers that can be obtained from C₄H₁₀ is
questions.explanation: C₄H₁₀ has 2 isomers: n-butane and 2-methylpropane (isobutane).

questions.question: The functional groups present in the compound above are
Which of the following is a primary amine?
Two organic compounds K and L were treated with a few drops of Fehling's solution respectively. K formed a brick red precipitate while L remains unaffected. The compound K is an
questions.explanation: Aldehydes (alkanals) give a brick-red Cu₂O precipitate with Fehling's solution; ketones do not.

questions.question: Which of the following statements is true about 2-methylpropane and butane?
options: They are members of the same homologous series
They have the same boiling point
They have different number of carbon atoms
They have the same chemical properties
questions.explanation: 2-methylpropane (isobutane) and butane are both alkanes with formula C₄H₁₀, belonging to the same homologous series (isomers).

questions.question: CH₃COOH + C₂H₅OH ⇌ CH₃COOC₂H₅ + H₂O in presence of H₂SO₄. The reaction above is best described as
questions.explanation: Esterification is the reaction between a carboxylic acid and an alcohol to form an ester and water.
